creative_brief: Clean, minimal design with emphasis on the 200kg figure and Rachel's Farm story. Include subtle visual of bulk ordering concept.
subject: We just bought 200kg of beef from one farm
preheader: Here's why that matters for your family's grocery bill
body:
Last Monday, we placed our biggest order yet with Rachel's Farm — 200kg of grass-fed beef from her property on the northern NSW coast.

Most families never get access to this. The supermarkets want consistency and shelf life over quality. Rachel's beef goes to restaurants or export because she can't afford the margins the big chains demand.

But when you buy in bulk — like we're doing — the economics flip. Rachel gets a fair price. Families get restaurant-quality beef at prices that actually make sense.

That's the investment thesis behind FarmThru. We're building the infrastructure to make farm-direct food accessible to everyday Sydney families, not just the wealthy suburbs.

No middlemen inflating prices. No 2,400km supply chains. Just farms like Rachel's connecting directly to families through our Brookvale hub.

We're raising capital to scale this model across Sydney. Minimum investment is $50, and we can't promise returns — but we can promise transparency about where your money goes.

Join our waitlist to get first access when the campaign opens.

This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal.
